prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_svr', seasonality_champion='DWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.89), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ONLY_CWT_svr dominates overall RMSE while DWT_svr excels at seasonality. Early/late horizon specialists differ substantially, supporting DBA combination which handles heterogeneous model behaviors optimally.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_CWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "late": "ONLY_CWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "THETA", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_svr", "ARIMA", "CWT_catboost", "ETS"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "rf", "DWT_catboost", "DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7231,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.89), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ONLY_CWT_svr dominates overall RMSE while DWT_svr excels at seasonality. Early/late horizon specialists differ substantially, supporting DBA combination which handles heterogeneous model behaviors optimally."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.43 (≥0.25) signals strong model disagreement requiring robust combination like dba_combination. RMSE gap between ONLY_CWT_svr (277.60) and catboost (480.74) is 73% spread, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.89). n_windows=3 requires small top_k (3) and adequate shrinkage (0.35) to avoid overfitting."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 3 different types (selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3; weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35; baseline: dba_combination) - good diversity. No leakage detected (inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage rolling selection). Universe leaderboards show no candidate beats all current candidates simultaneously (best_per_horizon_by_validation score 0.8625, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 score 0.8752, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 score 0.8767 are all in top rankings). No redundancy (4 candidates max, no ≥4 same-type). n_windows=3 meets weighted preference criteria. All names from valid_candidate_names.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.89), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ONLY_CWT_svr dominates overall RMSE while DWT_svr excels at seasonality. Early/late horizon specialists differ substantially, supporting DBA combination which handles heterogeneous model behaviors optimally.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.43 (≥0.25) signals strong model disagreement requiring robust combination like dba_combination. RMSE gap between ONLY_CWT_svr (277.60) and catboost (480.74) is 73% spread, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.89). n_windows=3 requires small top_k (3) and adequate shrinkage (0.35) to avoid overfitting.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 3 different types (selection: best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3; weighted: inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35; baseline: dba_combination) - good diversity. No leakage detected (inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage rolling selection). Universe leaderboards show no candidate beats all current candidates simultaneously (best_per_horizon_by_validation score 0.8625, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 score 0.8752, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 score 0.8767 are all in top rankings). No redundancy (4 candidates max, no ≥4 same-type). n_windows=3 meets weighted preference criteria. All names from valid_candidate_names.
